summary:
CR-6 Phase 2d G4 QA — ALL 3 sub-slices GREEN. (1) 2d.1 Solutions index hero CMS wiring: /solutions?admin=1 -> cms-username/cms-password/cms-login-submit -> cms-editbar appears WITH edit mode ON by default (2d.3 effect). All 4 pencils present: edit-solutions.index.hero.{eyebrow,h1,sub,cta}. Edited h1 via field-solutions.index.hero.h1 + save-solutions.index.hero.h1 (TextFieldModal flow) -> draft visible in admin view (h1='QA-SOL-H1-IT6') -> cms-publish -> fresh logged-out browser context renders the QA h1 with NO cms-editbar and NO edit-* pencils. (2) 2d.1 Product index hero CMS wiring: same flow on /product -> 4 pencils edit-product.index.hero.{eyebrow,h1,sub,cta}, container [data-testid=product-index-hero] and CTA [data-testid=product-index-hero-cta] both present. Round-trip 'QA-PROD-H1-IT6' -> publish -> verified in fresh logged-out context. (3) Logged-out fallback parity: PASSED. /solutions h1='One POS for every kind of hospitality business.'; /product h1='Every tool your business needs — in one operating system.'; both pages render with ZERO cms-editbar and ZERO edit-* pencils. (4) Hydration/DOM nesting: ZERO console errors and ZERO validateDOMNesting / '
cannot be a descendant of

' warnings across /solutions, /product, /solutions?admin=1, /product?admin=1, /pricing, /ai, /solutions/cafes, /product/sell-serve (errors+warnings captured count = 0). EditableText for hero sub uses multiline+rich without block (renders inline-block span wrapper inside

); h1 uses block (renders div wrapper inside h1 container) — correctly avoiding the div-in-p antipattern. (5) 2d.2 Sector proof-card data parity: /solutions/food-courts and /solutions/canteens now each render BOTH sector-proof-0 AND sector-proof-1 (verified in DOM with non-trivial content lengths 140/174 and 169/184). Backfill in /app/frontend/src/data/sectors.js confirmed (food-courts adds 'Phoenix Food Plaza' proof; canteens adds 'TechPark Central Canteen' proof). (6) 2d.3 CMS persist last-used mode (localStorage): VERIFIED end-to-end. (a) First login (empty localStorage) -> CmsProvider.login() sets editMode=true since savedEdit===null, confirmed by localStorage state {editMode:'true', preview:'false'} immediately after cms-login-submit and by pencils being immediately visible — fixes the discoverability issue flagged in iteration_5. (b) Click cms-toggle-preview ON -> localStorage flips to {editMode:'true', preview:'true'} -> page reload -> editbar reappears with pencils HIDDEN (preview mode persisted across reload). (c) Click cms-toggle-preview OFF -> pencils reappear -> page reload -> pencils still visible and localStorage={editMode:'true', preview:'false'} (edit mode persisted). Both persist effects in CmsProvider gated on `user` so they don't clobber pre-login defaults. (7) Regression — Phase 2a/2b/2c: /pricing renders with 8 edit-pricing.* pencils, /ai with 13 edit-ai.* pencils, /solutions/cafes with edit-sector.cafes.hero.h1 pencil, /product/sell-serve with edit-product.sell-serve.hero.h1 pencil — all still working in edit mode. (8) MANDATORY CLEANUP COMPLETED: ran mongosh deleteMany({key:/^(solutions|product)\.index\./}) -> deletedCount=2 (removed solutions.index.hero.h1 and product.index.hero.h1 QA overrides). Post-cleanup verification: GET /api/cms/content returns no solutions.index.* / product.index.* keys; logged-out /solutions and /product show original fallback H1s.
critical_code_review_comments: CmsProvider.jsx (Phase 2d.3): editMode/preview lazy-initialised from localStorage and persisted via two useEffect hooks gated on `user` — correct gating so logout doesn't write back. login() restores saved mode with first-login default editMode=true (savedEdit===null branch) and preview=(saved==='true'); this correctly addresses the iteration_5 UX concern about new admins seeing pencils-hidden. logout() clears in-memory state but does NOT clear localStorage cms.editMode/cms.preview — that's fine and intentional so the next login resumes the prior session's mode, but worth documenting.

SolutionsIndex.jsx + ProductIndex.jsx: hero sub uses WITHOUT block, which renders an inline wrapper inside the surrounding

tag — this avoids the div-in-p hydration warning. h1 uses block which renders a

wrapper but that's inside an

(h1 can contain block-level children OK in HTML5 spec for our case). 0 hydration warnings confirmed at runtime.
/app/frontend/src/data/sectors.js food-courts and canteens now each have 2 proof entries — closes the data-gap flagged in iteration_5. Quotes/clients are sensible and on-brand.
Product page CTA uses data-testid=product-index-hero-cta (singular, no '-index' duplication confusion) and hero container product-index-hero — matches the test spec exactly.

Minor (non-blocking): logout() in CmsProvider sets editMode=false and preview=false in state but the useEffect that persists them is gated on `user` so the localStorage write is skipped after logout — this means localStorage retains the previous user's last-used mode. Correct for resumption, but could be unexpected if multiple admins share a machine. Not a Phase 2d issue.
No backend changes in Phase 2d — backend untouched, all /api/cms/* endpoints behaving identically to iteration_5. Backend was not retested per 'frontend only' testing_type.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on all 7 critical Phase 2d flows + regression

test_credentials: CMS admin: username=admin / password=admin123. /app/memory/test_credentials.md is MISSING — flagged here for main agent to (re)create with the canonical CMS creds.
seed_data_creation: During the run published 2 keys: solutions.index.hero.h1='QA-SOL-H1-IT6' and product.index.hero.h1='QA-PROD-H1-IT6'. BOTH CLEANED UP at end via mongosh test_database deleteMany({key:/^(solutions|product)\.index\./}) -> deletedCount=2. Final state: GET /api/cms/content contains no solutions.index.* or product.index.* keys; fresh logged-out /solutions H1='One POS for every kind of hospitality business.' and /product H1='Every tool your business needs — in one operating system.'. Pre-existing pricing.* draft-only docs (no published_value, excluded from /api/cms/content) untouched — same safe baseline as end of iteration_5.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 2d CLOSED green. KEY TESTING NOTES for next agent: (1) After CmsProvider.login() with empty localStorage, editMode=true is the new default — pencils visible IMMEDIATELY (no cms-toggle-preview click needed). (2) Both /solutions and /product hero use the same wiring pattern: 4 separate keys eyebrow/h1/sub/cta with corresponding edit-<id> pencils that open TextFieldModal containing data-testid=field-<id> (input or textarea) and data-testid=save-<id> (save button). Use these testids — NOT generic textarea selectors — when scripting edits. (3) Visiting any page with ?admin=1 in URL re-opens the login modal even when already authenticated (CmsProvider.useState initialiser for loginOpen). For multi-page admin testing after login, navigate WITHOUT ?admin=1 to avoid the modal blocking pencil clicks. (4) localStorage keys cms.editMode and cms.preview persist across reloads while user is set; cleared in-memory on logout but the localStorage values are intentionally retained for resumption. (5) /solutions/food-courts and /solutions/canteens now have 2 proof cards (parity restored). (6) End-of-run cms_content state is clean — only pre-existing pricing.* drafts (no published) remain. Backend untouched in this iteration.
rca_of_the_issue: No P0/P1/P2 bugs found in Phase 2d. All 3 sub-slices implemented correctly and round-tripped end-to-end. The two iteration_5 minor observations (food-courts/canteens missing 2nd proof; CMS landing on Preview hiding pencils) are BOTH closed in this iteration via /app/frontend/src/data/sectors.js backfill and the /app/frontend/src/lib/cms/CmsProvider.jsx localStorage persistence with editMode=true default for new admins.
